description: Comparison infographic in portrait format on a dark black asphalt or concrete textured background with a faint ghosted open metal handcuff in the lower portion. The headline in white sans-serif reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Two side-by-side columns appear under colored banners: a red banner 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' (left) and a blue banner 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' (right), separated by a vertical divider. The left column has a large white numeral '8 to 10' with text 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' The right column has a large white '2' with text 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' The bottom-left has the DHS/ICE seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; the bottom-right has the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers'.
notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
